commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d3e14e1aa8732c28d3bd76f4580291f58b0c6985)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the doctor empty-allowlist scanner so a channel-level empty `groupAllowFrom` warning is suppressed when every active sub-account provides its own populated `groupAllowFrom`, and adds regression coverage for that shape.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +87. Total +113 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current source shows the top-level channel record is scanned before account records, and the proposed branch leaves a source-reproducible false-positive path through Telegram extra warnings and account-level `allowFrom` fallback.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the PR has concrete correctness gaps, mock-only proof, and competing sibling branches for the same linked issue.

target.title: fix(doctor): suppress false group-allowlist warning when all accounts override groupAllowFrom (fixes #92684)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `openclaw doctor` over-warns about an empty top-level `groupAllowFrom` when every sub-account defines its own populated `groupAllowFrom`. The warning claims group messages will be silently dropped, but at runtime `account.groupAllowFrom ?? parent.groupAllowFrom` means the empty top-level fallback is never consulted.
- **Root Cause**: `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` in `empty-allowlist-scan.ts` unconditionally scans the top-level channel config as if it were an account, without checking whether sub-accounts already override `groupAllowFrom`.
- **Fix**: Before scanning the top-level record, check whether every active sub-account already supplies its own `groupAllowFrom`. If all accounts override it, suppress the default group-allowlist warning for the top-level call (the skip is applied only when `parent` is undefined, so account-level checks are unaffected). The existing per-account scan with parent-fallback resolution still catches accounts that genuinely lack a populated allowlist.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.ts` — added pre-scan of sub-account `groupAllowFrom`, wrapped the channel-level `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning` hook when all accounts override
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - DM-policy (`dmPolicy: "allowlist"`) handling — the top-level DM warning is still emitted; only the group-allowlist false positive is addressed
  - Channel plugin custom skip hooks — the wrapped hook delegates to the original for non-top-level calls
  - Any runtime message-delivery logic — this is a doctor advisory fix only

### Reproduction

1. Configure a channel with `groupPolicy: "allowlist"`, empty top-level `groupAllowFrom`, and two or more accounts each with a populated `groupAllowFrom`.
2. Run `openclaw doctor`.
3. **Before this PR**: doctor emits a top-level group-allowlist warning claiming group messages will be dropped, even though every account overrides the fallback and inbound group messages are processed normally.
4. **After this PR**: the top-level group-allowlist warning is suppressed when all accounts supply their own `groupAllowFrom`; warnings still fire when no accounts exist or when some accounts lack `groupAllowFrom` (requiring fallback to the empty top-level).

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (92684)**: doctor `EmptyAllowlistCheck` warns the top-level record as if it were an account when `groupPolicy: "allowlist"` and `groupAllowFrom` is empty, even though every sub-account defines its own `groupAllowFrom`. The fix suppresses that false positive while preserving the warning when accounts genuinely lack a populated allowlist.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — project test harness with channel-capabilities resolution against `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan-92684.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)
   Start at  13:54:18
   Duration  786ms (transform 404ms, setup 382ms, import 96ms, tests 97ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 shard in 9.54s
```

**Observed result after fix**: the three new test scenarios confirm: (1) no top-level group-allowlist warning is emitted when two accounts each supply their own `groupAllowFrom` — the false positive from origin/main is eliminated; (2) the warning still fires when no accounts exist and the top-level is the only configuration; (3) the warning still fires when one account supplies `groupAllowFrom` but another does not, because the latter would fall back to the empty top-level.

**What was not tested**: live `openclaw doctor` CLI invocation against a real OpenClaw config file was not driven — the fix is validated through the existing `scanEmptyAllowlistPolicyWarnings` entry point with the project's channel-capabilities resolution harness.

**Repro confirmation**: the new "no top-level group warning when all accounts have their own groupAllowFrom" scenario fails on origin/main (returns a top-level group-allowlist warning, confirming the false positive) and succeeds after the patch (returns zero top-level group warnings).

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: the wrapped `shouldSkipDefaultEmptyGroupAllowlistWarning` is restored immediately after the top-level `checkAccount` call, but the function mutates `params` directly.
  **Mitigation**: the original function reference is saved before the wrap and unconditionally restored on the next line, so account-level checks always receive the original hook.
- **Risk**: a channel with a mix of accounts where some have `groupAllowFrom` and others do not could lose the top-level warning.
  **Mitigation**: the suppress condition requires **every** active sub-account to have a populated `groupAllowFrom` — a single account without it keeps the top-level warning intact.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI / doctor commands
- [x] Commands (doctor shared)

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-policy.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/empty-allowlist-scan-92684.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

N/A (initial submission)

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #92684
